steps: Vote to dissolve the LLC according to the operating agreement or by member consent
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
Apply for a Revenue Clearance Certificate from the Washington Department of Revenue
Receive the Revenue Clearance Certificate (DOR sends it directly to the Secretary of State)
File Certificate of Dissolution with the Secretary of State (no filing fee)
Submit the Revenue Clearance Certificate with the dissolution filing
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
File final state and federal tax returns
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
llc.notes: Washington does not charge a filing fee for dissolution. However, you must obtain a Revenue Clearance Certificate from the Department of Revenue before filing. Do not submit the dissolution without the actual Clearance Certificate — submitting only the application is not acceptable. 120 days after filing, the entity is permanently dissolved and ineligible for reinstatement.

steps: Hold a board of directors meeting to recommend dissolution
Obtain shareholder approval for the dissolution
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
Apply for a Revenue Clearance Certificate from the Washington Department of Revenue
Receive the Revenue Clearance Certificate (DOR sends it directly to the Secretary of State)
File Articles of Dissolution with the Secretary of State (no filing fee)
Submit the Revenue Clearance Certificate with the dissolution filing
Cancel any licenses, permits, and registrations
File final state and federal tax returns
Close business bank accounts and cancel your EIN with the IRS
corporation.notes: Washington does not charge a filing fee for dissolution. You must obtain a Revenue Clearance Certificate from the Department of Revenue before filing. Make checks or money orders payable to Secretary of State if any fees apply.

tax.notes: A Revenue Clearance Certificate from the Department of Revenue is required before filing dissolution papers. Apply through the Department of Revenue. They will send the certificate directly to the Secretary of State's office. Washington has no state income tax but has Business & Occupation (B&O) tax and sales tax that must be settled.

timeline.waitingPeriods: 120 days after dissolution filing, entity is permanently dissolved and ineligible for reinstatement

requirements.publicationDetails: Corporations must publish notice of dissolution within 30 days after the effective date of articles of dissolution (RCW 23B.14.030).
requirements.employeeNotification: Must provide WARN Act notice (60 days) if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors of the dissolution and provide a reasonable deadline for claims
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent designation terminates upon dissolution
requirements.windingUpPeriod: 120 days after filing, entity is permanently dissolved
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLC: Member vote as specified in operating agreement. Corporation: Board resolution followed by shareholder approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
